records.ety: [LL. implementum accomplishment, fr. L. implere, impletum, to fill up, finish, complete; pref. im- in + plere to fill. The word was perh. confused with OF. empleier, emploier, to employ, F. employer, whence E. employ. See Plenty.]

defs: That which fulfills or supplies a want or use; esp., an instrument, tool, or utensil, as supplying a requisite to an end; as, the implements of trade, of husbandry, or of war.
quotes: Genius must have talent as its complement and implement.
Revenge . . . executed and implemented by the hand of Vanbeest Brown.

defs: To provide with an implement or implements; to cause to be fulfilled, satisfied, or carried out, by means of an implement or implements.
quotes: The chief mechanical requisites of the barometer are implemented in such an instrument as the following.

defs: To fulfill or perform, as a contract or an engagement.
